fields.description:
# Enable public GitHub intake: Issues on, Go-style issue forms, SUPPORT.md rework

## Why

GitHub Issues are disabled on navapbc/rebar (verified via API 2026-07-09: `has_issues: false`), so outsiders have no zero-friction way to report bugs or request features — SUPPORT.md currently routes questions to email. Go demonstrates the convergent mirror pattern: code review on Gerrit, Issues canonical on GitHub — filing a bug requires zero Gerrit onboarding. Enabling Issues is the highest-leverage, lowest-cost community change; all three assessments converge on it.

## What

- Flip `has_issues=true` on navapbc/rebar (Settings or PATCH `/repos/navapbc/rebar`). Leave Discussions OFF (convergent single-maintainer advice: one inbox, no community to self-answer; issue→discussion conversion is one click if ever needed — decision recorded below).
- Rework `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` (bug_report.yml, feature_request.yml, config.yml exist today — rework in place) to Go-style numeric-ordered issue forms:
  - `00-bug.yml` — required rebar version, required environment textarea (`rebar --version`, OS, install method; `render: shell`), required did/saw/expected; `labels: ["bug", "needs-triage"]`.
  - `10-feature.yml` — problem statement + proposal textareas; `labels: ["enhancement", "needs-triage"]`.
  - `20-question.yml` — `labels: ["question"]`; unlike Go, questions stay IN Issues (rebar has no forum to redirect to).
- `config.yml`: `blank_issues_enabled: false` (no triage team to absorb free-form issues) + `contact_links`: (a) Gerrit — "Code changes / patches: this repo is a read-only mirror; code review happens on Gerrit, see CONTRIBUTING.md" → https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com; (b) Security — private reporting per SECURITY.md, never a public issue. The Gerrit contact_link is load-bearing: the chooser is exactly where a would-be PR author lands.
- Rework the existing `SUPPORT.md` (do NOT add a new one): re-route the "questions → email" path to GitHub Issues as primary; keep the Gerrit/security/CoC routing; keep the best-effort/no-SLA framing. Do not change the CoC enforcement contact line here — the governance child (feminine-goodly-koi) owns that edit; leave a consistent pointer.
- Create labels: `needs-triage`, `waiting-for-info`, `question`, and verify `bug`/`enhancement` exist (Go's label state machine, scaled down; stale-close automation deferred).

## Scope

IN: repo settings flip, `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/*` rework, SUPPORT.md rework (routing sections only), labels.
OUT: GitHub Issues ↔ rebar-store sync (separate epic, linked relates_to); enabling Discussions; stale-bot automation; issue-creation rate limiting (premature until abuse appears); the SUPPORT.md CoC contact line (governance child owns it).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `has_issues: true` live — prove: `gh api repos/navapbc/rebar --jq .has_issues` returns `true`; the rendered new-issue chooser shows 3 forms + 2 contact links and no blank-issue option.
- [ ] Bug form mechanically enforces version + environment + did/saw/expected — prove: `00-bug.yml` contains `render: shell` and three `validations: required: true` blocks; all forms declare their `labels:`.
- [ ] `SUPPORT.md` routes questions to GitHub Issues as the primary channel and retains mirror/Gerrit/security routing — prove: `grep -q "github.com/navapbc/rebar/issues" SUPPORT.md` and the email path is described as fallback, not primary.
- [ ] `config.yml` has `blank_issues_enabled: false` and both contact links — prove: `grep -c "url:" .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` ≥ 2.
- [ ] Labels exist — prove: `gh label list` includes needs-triage, waiting-for-info, question.
- [ ] Discussions-off decision recorded durably in-repo — prove: `grep -qi "discussions" SUPPORT.md` (one line stating Discussions are off, questions go to Issues, and the revisit trigger = question-issues clogging the tracker).
